reasoning: Reaching 15 billion cubic meters (bcm) in a single quarter requires an annualized import rate of 60 bcm. The background information confirms that pipeline deliveries through Ukraine (which typically carried about 14-15 bcm/year) were stopped in January 2025. The remaining active supply routes for Russian gas into the EU are the TurkStream pipeline (whose capacity dedicated to Europe is roughly 15.75 bcm/year) and LNG imports (which reached a historic maximum of around 18-20 bcm/year for the EU). Combined, these routes can supply at most about 8 to 9 bcm per quarter if operating at full capacity. Without the Nord Stream pipelines (destroyed) or the Yamal-Europe pipeline (sanctioned), there is simply not enough physical infrastructure to transport 15 bcm of Russian gas to the EU in a single quarter. The crowd forecast is at 1%, acknowledging this physical limit. Consequently, the probability of hitting 15 bcm in any quarter of 2025 is exceedingly close to zero. I am submitting the minimum permitted probability of 2%.
